steps.title: Break down the pricing
steps.content:
First 25 people: We charge \$28 per person, so the cost is:
$$25 \times 28 = 700$$

Additional people: We charge \$17 per person. If the group has $n$ people (where $n \geq 25$), then there are $(n - 25)$ additional people.

steps.title: Build and simplify the function
steps.content:
Total charge = Cost of first 25 + Cost of additional people
$$f(n) = 700 + 17(n - 25)$$

Expand and simplify:
$$f(n) = 700 + 17n - 425$$
$$f(n) = 17n + 275$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
